summary: Health Companion is a comprehensive health insurance policy that covers Allopathic and AYUSH treatments taken in India only. The policy provides coverage for hospitalization expenses, pre and post hospitalization expenses, ambulance charges, modern treatments, organ donor expenses, and includes benefits like No Claim Bonus, Refill benefit, and health checkup. Optional benefits include Hospital Cash and Personal Accident coverage.
coverage: Ambulance expenses: By road maximum Rs.2,000 & by air maximum Rs.2,50,000 per hospitalization
Hospitalization expenses for admission of 2 hours or more (minimum 24 hours for AYUSH Hospital)
Dialysis (Hemo/Peritoneal), Radiotherapy or Chemotherapy for cancer
Modern treatments including Uterine Artery Embolization, HIFU, Immunotherapy, Robotic surgeries, etc.
Health checkup once every policy year from day 1
Vaccination for animal bite up to Rs.5,000
Optional Hospital Cash benefit for up to 30 days
Optional Personal Accident coverage including Accidental Death and Disability benefits
waiting_periods: Pre-existing diseases: 36 months of continuous coverage
Specified disease/procedure waiting period: 24 months of continuous coverage
30-day waiting period for any illness from first policy commencement date
Personal waiting period: Up to 48 months for conditions specified in policy schedule
room_rent: For Variant 2023: Room type capping up to Shared Room
20% co-payment applies for higher room category in Variant 2023
co_pay: 20% co-payment applies for room categories higher than Shared Room in Variant 2023
deductible: Aggregate amount in a year incurred by insured on hospital admission which insurer will not pay
Once total expense exceeds deductible amount, balance will be paid by insurer
Deductible does not apply to Health Check-up, Vaccination for Animal Bite and Hospital Cash benefits
claims_information: Company shall settle or reject claim within 15 days from claim submission date
For delay in payment, company liable to pay interest at 2% above bank rate
Cashless facility available at network providers
Claims require admission and treatment, not just investigations
Health checkup must be booked through digital assets (Mobile App) - cashless only
exclusions: Pre-existing diseases until 36 months of continuous coverage
Specified diseases/procedures until 24 months
30-day waiting period for illness from first policy start
Investigation and evaluation expenses for diagnostic purposes only
Rest cure, rehabilitation and respite care
Obesity/weight control treatments (unless meeting specific conditions)
Cosmetic or plastic surgery (unless medically necessary)
Hazardous or adventure sports injuries
Treatment arising from breach of law with criminal intent
Alcoholism, drug or substance abuse treatment
Refractive error treatment less than 7.5 dioptres
Sterility and infertility treatments
Maternity expenses
Circumcision (unless medically necessary)
War, terrorism, nuclear incidents
External congenital anomaly
Dental/oral treatment (except due to accident during hospitalization)
Medical devices for home use
OPD treatment expenses
Intentional self-inflicted injury or suicide attempts

faqs.question: What is the coverage area for Health Companion policy?
faqs.answer: This policy covers Allopathic and AYUSH treatments taken in India ONLY. Expenses incurred outside India are not covered.

faqs.question: What happens to unutilized sum insured at the end of policy year?
faqs.answer: Unutilized Sum Insured will expire at the end of policy year.

faqs.question: What is the minimum admission period required for hospitalization coverage?
faqs.answer: Admission for 2 hours or more is required. However, for AYUSH treatment, minimum 24 hours admission in AYUSH Hospital is mandatory.

faqs.question: Are investigation-only admissions covered?
faqs.answer: No, we will NOT pay if there was no treatment and only investigations were done, such as admission only for MRI, CT Scan, Endoscopy, Colonoscopy etc.

faqs.question: What is the ambulance coverage limit?
faqs.answer: By road maximum Rs.2,000 and by air maximum Rs.2,50,000 per hospitalization. You must use a registered ambulance/air ambulance provider.

faqs.question: When is air ambulance coverage available?
faqs.answer: Air ambulance is available only for Emergency care.

faqs.question: What is the pre-hospitalization coverage period?
faqs.answer: We will pay expenses incurred 60 days before date of admission if these are related to the condition for which hospital admission claim is paid.

faqs.question: What is the post-hospitalization coverage period?
faqs.answer: We will pay expenses incurred 180 days after date of discharge if these are related to the condition for which hospital admission claim is paid.

faqs.question: How does the No Claim Bonus work?
faqs.answer: For every claim free year, we will add 20% of expiring policy base sum insured as NCB, maximum up to 100%.

faqs.question: What is the Refill benefit?
faqs.answer: We will add an amount equal to the base sum insured after the first claim is paid. This benefit applies only once in a policy year and is triggered even at partial utilization of base sum insured.

faqs.question: When is health checkup benefit available?
faqs.answer: Health checkup is available once every Policy Year, from day 1 of the policy. Tests must be booked through digital assets (Mobile App) and are available only on cashless basis.

faqs.question: What is covered under vaccination for animal bite?
faqs.answer: Vaccination required post an animal bite is covered up to Rs.5,000.

faqs.question: What is the waiting period for pre-existing diseases?
faqs.answer: Pre-existing diseases are excluded until expiry of 36 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first policy.

faqs.question: What is the waiting period for specified diseases?
faqs.answer: Specified diseases/procedures are excluded until expiry of 24 months of continuous coverage. This exclusion does not apply for claims arising due to accident or cancer (covered after 30-day waiting period).

faqs.question: What is the 30-day waiting period?
faqs.answer: Treatment of any illness within 30 days from first policy commencement date is excluded except claims arising due to accident. This does not apply if insured has continuous coverage for more than twelve months.

faqs.question: What is the room rent restriction for Variant 2023?
faqs.answer: For Variant 2023, there is room type capping up to Shared Room. 20% co-payment will apply in case of higher room category.

faqs.question: Are modern treatments covered?
faqs.answer: Yes, modern treatments are covered including Uterine Artery Embolization, HIFU, Immunotherapy, Robotic surgeries, Stem cell therapy for bone marrow transplant, and others as specified in the policy.

faqs.question: Is domiciliary treatment covered?
faqs.answer: Yes, home care treatment is covered when medical practitioner advises treatment at home with continuous monitoring and daily treatment records maintained by treating doctor.

faqs.question: What is the Hospital Cash benefit?
faqs.answer: Hospital Cash is an optional benefit that pays a fixed amount for each day of hospitalization, maximum up to 30 days, applicable only when admitted for 48 hours or more continuously.

faqs.question: What does Personal Accident coverage include?
faqs.answer: Personal Accident is optional and includes Accidental Death benefit and Permanent Total/Partial Disability benefits with specific percentages based on type of disability.

faqs.question: What is the free look period?
faqs.answer: Free look period is 30 days from date of receipt of policy document to review terms and conditions. If not satisfied, policy can be cancelled with refund of premium subject to deductions.

faqs.question: Can the policy be cancelled?
faqs.answer: Yes, policyholder may cancel policy anytime by giving 7 days' notice in writing. Proportionate premium refund available if no claims made during policy period.

faqs.question: What is the grace period for renewal?
faqs.answer: Grace period is 30 days for annual premium and 15 days for monthly premium. Coverage is available during grace period if premium is paid.

faqs.question: What is the moratorium period?
faqs.answer: After completion of 60 continuous months of coverage, no policy and claim shall be contestable on grounds of non-disclosure or misrepresentation, except for established fraud.

faqs.question: Are maternity expenses covered?
faqs.answer: No, medical treatment expenses traceable to childbirth including complicated deliveries and caesarean sections are excluded, except ectopic pregnancy.

faqs.question: Is dental treatment covered?
faqs.answer: Dental/oral treatment is excluded except if required by insured person while hospitalized due to an accident.

faqs.question: Are OPD expenses covered?
faqs.answer: No, any expenses incurred on OPD treatment are excluded from coverage.

faqs.question: What happens in case of multiple policies?
faqs.answer: For indemnity policies, insured can choose primary insurer for settlement. If claim exceeds coverage, primary insurer coordinates with other insurers for balance amount settlement.

faqs.question: What is migration facility?
faqs.answer: Migration allows transfer of credits like Sum Insured, No Claim Bonus, waiting periods from one policy to another with same insurer, subject to underwriting if not continuously covered for 36 months.

faqs.question: What is portability?
faqs.answer: Portability allows transfer of policy from one insurer to another with transfer of credits like Sum Insured, No Claim Bonus, waiting periods, subject to board approved underwriting policy.
